one_line_definition: Heat flux is proportional to the negative temperature gradient: q = -k·dT/dx
Material property quantifying how readily heat flows through a substance (W/m·K)
Analogue of electrical resistance for heat flow: R = L/(kA) for a slab; enables resistance circuits
Dimensionless ratio of convective to conductive resistance: Bi = hL/k; governs whether a body can be treated as lumped
Dimensionless convective heat transfer coefficient: Nu = hL/k_fluid
Relation linking skin friction coefficient to Stanton number: St·Pr^(2/3) = c_f/2
Ratio of momentum diffusivity to thermal diffusivity: Pr = μ·c_p/k
Dimensionless ratio of inertial to viscous forces in a flow: Re = ρuL/μ
Dimensionless heat transfer coefficient: St = h/(ρ·u·c_p) = Nu/(Re·Pr)
Ratio of actual fin heat transfer to the ideal (if entire fin were at base temperature): η = tanh(mL)/(mL)
Simplification assuming spatially uniform body temperature; valid when Bi < 0.1
Heat exchanger sizing method relating effectiveness to NTU = UA/C_min
Effective driving temperature difference for heat exchanger design
Radiated power per unit area from a blackbody: q = σT^4 (σ = 5.67×10^-8 W/m²K^4)
Ratio of actual radiation emitted by a surface to that of a blackbody at the same temperature
For a body in thermal equilibrium, absorptance equals emittance: α = ε
Fraction of radiation leaving surface i that strikes surface j; depends only on geometry
Iterative structural analysis technique using a secant (chord) modulus to handle non-linear material behaviour
Curvature induced in a beam by a non-uniform temperature gradient across the cross-section via differential thermal expansion
Three-parameter equation describing non-linear stress-strain curves; used here for steel at elevated temperatures
Location within a heated cross-section where net internal force is zero; shifts from centroid when temperature is non-uniform
Temperature distribution across a structural cross-section that varies with depth, idealised as discrete constant-temperature strips
Ability of a structural member to maintain load-carrying capacity under prescribed fire exposure conditions
Standardised time-temperature relationship used in fire resistance testing of structural elements
Steel beam type where the floor slab sits on the bottom flange, shielding most of the section from fire
Steel beam type where angles bolted to the web support the slab, significantly shielding the upper web from fire
Temperature-dependent property tabulated in TPRC Vol. 1 for hundreds of metallic elements and engineering alloys
Critically evaluated multi-volume reference compiling thermophysical property data compiled at Purdue University
